uses: Hemicarpus glandulosus, commonly known as the false nutmeg, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. intro: Hemicarpus glandulosus, also known as Indian snakeroot, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemicarpus glandulosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Hemicarpus glandulosus is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
